posts.title: 🎤 Please join PVHS & TKJHS for a Motivational Keynote Presentation from Erica Alfaro 4/13/26 @ 6PM in the PVHS Library
posts.summary: Motivational speaker Erica Alfaro will be presenting at PVHS on Monday 4/13/26 at 6pm in the PVHS Library. PVHS, in partnership with TKJHS, is excited to offer our School Community a chance to hear an inspirational message.
posts.author: Paul Robinson
posts.body:
Motivational speaker Erica Alfaro will be presenting at PVHS on Monday 4/13/26 at 6pm in the PVHS Library.

PVHS, in partnership with TKJHS, is very excited to offer our School Community a chance to hear an inspirational message from Erica Alfaro. Erica is an award-winning author, keynote speaker, and educational advocate. In 2019, Erica's graduation pictures went viral and made headlines around the world. She was featured in major media outlets such as CNN, The Huffington Post, and Yahoo News.

Erica's Harvesting Dreams book is an empowering, inspirational and motivational story. It is a testimony of a girl who went through many adversities and managed to reinvent herself and become the woman she is today. Erica's book won the 2022 International Latino Book Award in the Most Inspirational Young Adult category.

Don't miss this wonderful opportunity to be inspired!

posts.title: 📬 PVHS Weekly Newsletter 4/2/2026
posts.summary: Weekly update from PVHS for April 2, 2026. Spring Break is here — no classes from Friday 4/3/26 to Friday 4/10/26. We return on Monday 4/13/26.
posts.author: Araceli Mandujano-Padilla
posts.body:
WEEKLY UPDATE — PIONEER VALLEY HIGH SCHOOL
April 2, 2026

Hello Panthers — Welcome to this week's edition of the Pioneer Valley Newsletter!

Paul R. Robinson, Ph.D. — Principal

Spring Break: Spring Break is here! There will be no classes from Friday 4/3/26 to Friday 4/10/26. We return on Monday 4/13/26. As we head into Spring Break, I want to wish all of our students and staff a well-deserved time to rest, recharge, and enjoy the season.

Guest Speaker 4/13: Motivational speaker Erica Alfaro will be presenting at PVHS on Monday 4/13/26 at 6pm in the PVHS Library. Erica is an award-winning author, keynote speaker, and educational advocate. Her book Harvesting Dreams won the 2022 International Latino Book Award in the Most Inspirational Young Adult category. Don't miss this wonderful opportunity to be inspired!

Merrie Okie-Goldin — Assistant Principal, Special Programs

The Rotary Club Essay Contest: Congratulations to Zaira Nacar for submitting a winning essay in the Santa Maria Breakfast Rotary's 4-Way Test Essay Contest. She will have the honor of reading her winning essay to the Rotary group on April 30th.

CAASPP Testing Begins April 16: When we return from break, our CAASPP state testing window will begin, starting with Science (CAST) on April 16. For juniors, strong performance on the 11th-grade CAASPP English and Math assessments may be used by California community colleges and CSU campuses as part of college course placement. Students who meet or exceed the standard may be able to start in college-level courses, saving time and money. We encourage students to: get a good night's sleep, eat breakfast, take their time, and show what they know.

Jeffrey Hromadka — Assistant Principal, Student Services

Senior Event: PVHS wrapped up the week with an exciting day! Seniors gathered for a senior meeting, captured memories with a panoramic picture, and received their graduation gowns — all hosted by Mrs. Walters.

Kevin Ilac — Assistant Principal, Instruction

Junior Financial Aid Presentations: Our counselors met with all 11th grade students this week to highlight the importance of applying for financial aid. A detailed and informative presentation was shared with the junior class to help guide them through the process.

Spring Faire: We celebrated with our annual Spring Faire, where our clubs came together to sell items and fundraise for their organizations. Thank you to our students and staff for making the day fun-filled, productive, and seamless.

Christian Basulto — Dean of Students

BLE Cycle Assessment: As part of the next step in the Curriculum Alignment Project (CAP), the Biology of the Living Earth (BLE) team from all three comprehensive sites came together to begin creating cycle assessments. Using the platforms Inner Orbit and Gemini, teachers collaborated to design questions with a balanced mix of 1D, 2D, and 3D assessment items, ensuring students are evaluated on content knowledge, application, and deeper scientific thinking. Great work to our Biology and Ag Biology teachers!

posts.title: 🎓 Meet with an Allan Hancock College Counselor at PVHS Tuesday, April 21st
posts.summary: If you're planning on attending Allan Hancock College in the fall and haven't met with an AHC Counselor yet, reserve your appointment for Tuesday, April 21st to get your First Semester Plan.
posts.author: Melani Mederos
posts.body:
Hey Panthers!

If you're planning on attending Allan Hancock College in the fall, and you haven't met with an AHC Counselor yet, scan the QR code below to reserve your appointment time for Tuesday, April 21st.

You must meet with an AHC Counselor to get your "First Semester Plan" so you can register for classes in May!

Questions? Please see your PVHS Counselor, Mr. Hernandez in the library, or Mrs. Mederos in the College & Career Center (room 604).

posts.title: 🚨 Campus Update
posts.summary: About noon today, a male PVHS student had a medical emergency during class. A school nurse immediately responded and the student was transported by ambulance to the hospital for follow-up.
posts.author: Paul Robinson
posts.body:
Good Afternoon PV School Community,

About noon today, a male PVHS student had a medical emergency during class. A school nurse immediately responded to the classroom and the student was transported by ambulance to the hospital for follow-up.

Counseling services are always made available during these types of concerning situations.

Respectfully,
Paul Robinson, Principal

posts.title: 🎪 Spring Fair, Minimum Day, & Important Senior Updates! Tomorrow 4/2
posts.summary: Tomorrow is an action-packed day heading into Spring Break! Minimum Day ends at 1:35 PM, Spring Fair during lunch, and major senior events during Periods 3 and 4 including panoramic picture and cap & gown distribution.
posts.author: Jeffrey Hromadka
posts.body:
Dear Families and Students,

We have an action-packed day tomorrow as we head into our Spring Break! Please review the following updates regarding tomorrow's schedule and upcoming events.

1. Minimum Day Schedule
Tomorrow will be a minimum day. School will conclude after Period 7 at 1:35 PM. Please ensure transportation arrangements are made for this earlier dismissal time.

2. Spring Fair Tomorrow
Get ready for some fun! We are hosting our Spring Fair tomorrow during lunch. Our school clubs will be out in force selling a variety of food and treats. It's going to be a great day to enjoy the outdoors and support our student organizations.

3. Essential Updates for Seniors (Class of 2026)
Tomorrow is a major milestone day for our seniors! Activities will take place during Periods 3 and 4:

  • Panoramic Picture: Seniors will head to the stands for their official group photo.
  • Graduation Rehearsal: We will conduct our first graduation walkthrough.
  • Cap & Gown Distribution: Jostens will be on-site to hand out graduation gowns immediately following the photo.

Attendance is critical. We want every senior to be included in the picture and to receive their graduation gear on time!

4. Spring Break Reminder
Spring Break officially begins this Friday, April 3 (No School). Classes will resume on Monday, April 13.

posts.title: 📬 PVHS Weekly Newsletter 3/27/2026
posts.summary: Weekly update from PVHS for March 27, 2026. Highlights include Fiddler on the Roof opening night, ELPAC testing wrap-up, Spring Fair preview, and Diablo Canyon Nuclear Power Plant field trip.
posts.author: Araceli Mandujano-Padilla
posts.body:
WEEKLY UPDATE — PIONEER VALLEY HIGH SCHOOL
March 27, 2026

Hello Panthers — Welcome to this week's edition of the Pioneer Valley Newsletter!

Paul R. Robinson, Ph.D. — Principal

Fiddler on the Roof: PVHS Center Stage rocked the Clark Center with their opening night performance of Fiddler on the Roof. Guest Artist J.J. Niemann, who has performed in Hamilton and other Broadway productions, joined PVHS performers for a set after the play. Upcoming performances include March 27 at 7:00pm and March 28 at 2:00pm and 7:00pm at the Clark Center, as well as April 16, 17, and 18 at 7:00pm in the Performing Arts Center. Read the KSBY coverage.

Hiring Update: PVHS has hired two International Language (Spanish) Teachers and one Science teacher. We still have multiple Special Education Mild Moderate (MMSN) openings. Apply here.

Merrie Okie-Goldin — Assistant Principal, Special Programs

ELPAC Testing Reminder: We are wrapping up our ELPAC testing and still need a few students to complete their test. The ELPAC (English Language Proficiency Assessments for California) measures how well students listen, speak, read, and write in English. Every student's participation matters.

Jeffrey Hromadka — Assistant Principal, Student Services

Kick off your Spring Break with a bang at our Spring Fair next Thursday during lunch! Our seniors will also gather for the Senior Graduation Assembly during periods 3 and 4, including official panoramic and class year photos.

Kevin Ilac — Assistant Principal, Instruction

Our school counselors and Career Center Specialist recently visited 9th, 10th, and 11th grade classrooms to help students navigate the CCGI (California Colleges Guidance Initiative) digital platform. PVHS also hosted the ASVAB military aptitude test with over 25 students in attendance.

This week, Melani Mederos, College & Career Center Specialist, was honored by the U.S. Army Gold Coast Recruiting Company for her outstanding partnership and support.

Christian Basulto — Dean of Students

Diablo Canyon Nuclear Power Plant: On March 25th, students from Mr. Magni's AP Environmental Science class had the unique opportunity to visit the Diablo Canyon Nuclear Power Plant, located just north of Avila Beach. The facility had not allowed student visits for the past few years and only recently reopened its doors to schools. Students engaged directly with scientists and support staff who help operate a plant that provides nearly 10% of California's power. A special thank you to Mr. Magni, Ms. Mihelic, and Mrs. Knowlson for arranging this amazing field trip.

posts.title: 🏈 Mandatory Football Meeting for 9th Graders
posts.summary: Calling all 9th graders ready to suit up for football next season! Mandatory meeting on Tuesday, March 31 at 12:35 PM in Room 551. Bring your lunch and your A game.
posts.author: Jeffrey Hromadka
posts.body:
Calling all 9th graders ready to suit up for football next season! If you want to hit the field next fall, this is the meeting you can't miss (meaning it's mandatory).

  • When: Tuesday, March 31
  • Time: 12:35 PM (Don't be late!)
  • Where: Room 551 (Right by the cafeteria)
  • What to bring: Your lunch and your "A" game.

Let's get the season started right. See you there!
Coach Payne

posts.title: 🎓 Senior Meeting — next Thursday, April 2
posts.summary: CLASS OF 2026 — Next Thursday, April 2, there will be a senior class meeting beginning during 3rd period. Panoramic and class year pictures will be taken, and cap and gown pickup for those who ordered.
posts.author: Lisa Walters
posts.body:
CLASS OF 2026!!

Next Thursday, April 2, there will be a senior class meeting, beginning during 3rd period. You will go to your 3rd period class and then be dismissed to the gym.

During the meeting, you will have your panoramic and class year pictures taken, will receive information about end of the year events, and if you have already ordered your cap and gown — you will pick it up at the end of the meeting.

Information about the distribution of the pictures will be shared after the meeting.

If you haven't ordered your cap and gown, please go to jostens.com to order.

Reminder: Caps cannot be decorated or in any way modified.
